topic: How to turn one piece of content into ten
format: Open with a specific campaign, launch, or test that failed — and exactly what it revealed
post:
We spent $8K promoting a long-form report. 200 downloads. Painful.

The content was good. The distribution was lazy.

Same research. We chopped it into 11 assets over 6 weeks — LinkedIn carousels, a cold email sequence, a 90-second Reel, three newsletter blurbs, a webinar hook. Total extra spend: $0.

That campaign drove 4x the pipeline of the original launch.

Here's the actual breakdown of one piece → ten:

1 long-form piece (report, essay, case study)
→ 3 short social posts pulling one stat each
→ 1 carousel (the framework or the findings)
→ 1 email to your list (the counterintuitive angle)
→ 1 short video (your hottest take from it)
→ 1 sales enablement slide
→ 1 FAQ thread or comment reply bank
→ 1 podcast talking point
→ 1 retargeting ad pulling the sharpest quote
→ 1 follow-up post 30 days later ("what we got wrong")

Most teams publish once and move on.

The teams that win publish once and mine it for 60 days.

Distribution isn't a bonus step. It is the strategy.

What's the longest you've ever stretched a single piece of content — and what format surprised you most?
